title: cirsium lanceolatum
intro: Cirsium lanceolatum, also known as the lance-leaved thistle or tall thistle, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cirsium lanceolatum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Herbal preparations of Cirsium lanceolatum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
